Title: A J-Protein OsDjC46 Interacts with ZFP36 to Participate in ABA-Mediated Antioxidant Defense in Rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsDjC46 (LOC_Os04g59060) belongs to the third category of J-proteins in rice and is part of a large J-protein family. Its expression pattern shows two ABA- and H2O2-dependent induction peaks, suggesting feedback regulation involving ABA biosynthesis and ROS generation previously attributed to ZFP36. OsDjC46 is proposed to participate in ABA-induced H2O2 production and Ca2+-mediated ROS signaling, acting together with ZFP36 in the nucleus to modulate antioxidant defense.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDjC46 was identified as a ZFP36-interacting J-protein (DnaJ/Hsp40) in rice by yeast two-hybrid screening and confirmed to interact with ZFP36 in vivo and in vitro (Y2H, BiFC, Co-IP, GST pull-down). 2. OsDjC46 expression in rice leaves is rapidly induced by ABA, H2O2 and Ca2+, and is upregulated by drought and salt stress (20% PEG6000, 150 mM NaCl); ABA-induced expression depends on ABA-triggered H2O2 and Ca2+ signaling. 3. OsDjC46 is localized in the nucleus of rice protoplasts, consistent with its interaction with the nuclear transcription factor ZFP36. 4. Overexpression of OsDjC46 enhances, whereas CRISPR/Cas9 knockout reduces, drought and salt tolerance in rice, as evidenced by altered SOD and CAT activities, MDA content, electrolyte leakage, relative water content, proline accumulation, survival rate and growth parameters under PEG and NaCl treatments. 5. These results indicate that OsDjC46 positively regulates rice drought and salt tolerance by participating in ABA-mediated antioxidant defense via interaction with ZFP36.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsDjC46 positively regulates rice tolerance to drought and salt stress by enhancing ABA-mediated antioxidant defense, leading to higher SOD and CAT activities, improved osmotic adjustment and reduced cell membrane damage under PEG and NaCl treatments.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsDjC46 is a nuclear J-protein (DnaJ/Hsp40) that interacts physically with the zinc finger transcription factor ZFP36 and is induced by ABA, H2O2 and Ca2+ signals; it modulates antioxidant defense by affecting SOD and CAT activities and proline accumulation, thereby limiting ROS-induced membrane damage under drought and salt stress, although the precise biochemical mechanism remains unresolved.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ABA signaling and ROS- and Ca2+-mediated stress-response pathways, participating in ABA-induced antioxidant defense under drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ABA-mediated antioxidant defense and ROS-scavenging pathway under drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: J-protein (DnaJ) of the Hsp40 family, classified as a type C J-protein with a C-terminal J-domain.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus: 35S:OsDjC46-YFP fusion transiently expressed in rice protoplasts shows nuclear localization, co-localizing with the nuclear dye DAPI.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZFP36 (C2H2 zinc finger transcription factor); interaction demonstrated by yeast two-hybrid, BiFC in onion epidermal cells, Co-IP in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and GST pull-down assays in vitro.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsDjC46 expression in rice leaves is rapidly and transiently induced by H2O2 (first peak at ~10 min, second peak at ~60 min) and by ABA (two peaks, at ~15 min and 60 min), and is upregulated within 4–5 h under drought (20% PEG6000) and salt (150 mM NaCl) stress. OsDjC46 expression is also induced by CaCl2, with a maximum around 180 min. ABA-induced OsDjC46 expression is abolished when plants are pretreated with H2O2 scavengers/inhibitors (DMTU, CAT, DPI) or Ca2+-related blockers and antagonists (EGTA, TFP, W7, KN-93), indicating dependence on ABA-triggered ROS and Ca2+ signaling.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abscisic acid (100 μmol·L^-1 ABA); hydrogen peroxide (10 mmol·L^-1 H2O2); CaCl2 (10 mM); drought stress simulated by 20% PEG6000; salt stress simulated by 150 mM NaCl; H2O2 scavengers and inhibitor (DMTU, CAT, DPI); Ca2+-related blockers and antagonists (EGTA, TFP, W7, W5 control, KN-93, KN-92 control).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and salt); ABA-mediated antioxidant defense; ROS signaling in rice.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress: drought tolerance; salt tolerance; oxidative stress response and antioxidant defense.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Compared with wild type under 20% PEG6000 and 150 mM NaCl, OsDjC46 knockout plants display reduced SOD and CAT activities and increased MDA content, electrolyte leakage and decreased relative water content and proline, whereas OsDjC46-overexpression plants show higher SOD and CAT activities, lower MDA and electrolyte leakage, higher relative water content and proline, and higher survival rate, fresh weight, plant height and root length (values summarized qualitatively based on Figures 3 and 4).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under drought and salt stress, OsDjC46-overexpression plants show lower MDA content and electrolyte leakage, higher relative water content and proline accumulation, higher survival rate after stress and recovery, and greater plant height, fresh weight and root length compared with wild type; OsDjC46 knockout mutants show the opposite trends, with more severe leaf yellowing and curling and reduced growth under PEG and NaCl treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa L. sub. japonica cv. Nipponbare wild-type seedlings; OsDjC46-overexpression rice lines (pSUPER1300-OsDjC46); OsDjC46 CRISPR/Cas9 knockout rice lines; rice leaf protoplasts; Nicotiana benthamiana and Nicotiana tabacum leaves for transient expression; onion (Allium cepa L.) epidermal cells; Escherichia coli for recombinant protein expression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Yeast two-hybrid screening and directed Y2H interaction assays; BiFC in onion epidermal cells; Co-IP in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; GST pull-down assay in vitro; construction of OsDjC46 overexpression and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout lines via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation; RT-qPCR analysis of OsDjC46 expression under ABA, H2O2, CaCl2, PEG and NaCl and with ROS- and Ca2+-related inhibitors; subcellular localization using 35S:OsDjC46-YFP in rice protoplasts; drought and salt stress treatments using 20% PEG6000 and 150 mM NaCl; biochemical assays of SOD and CAT activities, MDA content, electrolyte leakage, relative water content and proline content; phenotypic measurements of survival rate, plant height, fresh weight and root length under stress.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Generation and analysis of OsDjC46 overexpression and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout rice lines under drought (20% PEG6000) and salt (150 mM NaCl) stress, including survival and growth phenotypes plus SOD and CAT activities, MDA, electrolyte leakage, relative water content and proline content; supported by interaction validation with ZFP36 via Y2H, BiFC, Co-IP and GST pull-down, and subcellular localization of OsDjC46-YFP in rice protoplast nuclei.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a ZFP36-interacting protein by yeast two-hybrid screening of a rice leaf cDNA library using full-length ZFP36 as bait (GAL4-BD fusion), followed by confirmation of interaction in additional Y2H assays. Full-length OsDjC46 cDNA was cloned from a rice cDNA library for subcellular localization and functional studies, and CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing was used to generate OsDjC46 knockout lines; an overexpression construct (OsDjC46 fused into pSUPER1300 under 35S promoter) was used to create overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsDjC46 is a nuclear-localized J-protein whose overexpression enhances, and knockout reduces, tolerance of rice seedlings to drought and salt stress by strengthening ABA-mediated antioxidant defense (higher SOD and CAT activities, higher proline and relative water content, lower MDA and electrolyte leakage, and better growth under PEG and NaCl). OsDjC46 is therefore a promising target gene for breeding or engineering rice with improved drought and salinity tolerance through transgenic approaches or marker-assisted selection focused on ABA–ROS signaling and antioxidant capacity.
